scenario: Velocity SaaS runs a Camunda 8.8 onboarding process for 7500 customers per month. The architect must decide whether to model the dunning policy with conditional events or replace them with timer events, and so checks the BPMN coverage events table to see which conditional event variants are natively implemented for Camunda 8.8.
question: Which conditional event variants are marked as implemented in the BPMN coverage events table for Camunda 8.8?

options.text: Only conditional start event and conditional catch event variants are marked as implemented for Camunda 8.8
Only conditional boundary event and conditional event subprocess variants are marked as implemented for Camunda 8.8
All conditional variants except throw and end are marked as implemented for Camunda 8.8 in the events table
No conditional event variant is marked as implemented for Camunda 8.8 in the BPMN coverage events table

a.text: Incorrect. The conditional start event and the conditional catch event shapes are rendered with a placeholder link and without the implemented class on the BPMN coverage events table.

b.text: Incorrect. The conditional boundary event and the conditional event subprocess shapes are rendered without the implemented class and link only to a placeholder anchor.

c.text: Incorrect. Every conditional cell on the BPMN coverage events table uses a placeholder link without the implemented class, including the start, catch, boundary, and event subprocess shapes.

d.text: Correct. The whole conditional row on the BPMN coverage events table renders every shape with a placeholder anchor and no implemented class, so no conditional event variant is natively supported in Camunda 8.8.

explanation: Conditional events are drawn on the BPMN coverage events page so they appear in the palette, but no conditional cell carries the implemented class. None of the conditional event variants are natively supported in Camunda 8.8.
